algorithms.title: Rescue Breathing (Breaths) for a Patient with a Pulse
algorithms.description: The sequence for assisting a patient who is unresponsive with a pulse but inadequate or absent breathing.
steps: Confirm the patient is unresponsive with a pulse but not breathing normally.
Open the airway (head-tilt/chin-lift, or jaw-thrust if trauma is suspected).
Deliver breaths at the age-appropriate rate: adult 1 breath every 6 seconds (~10/min); infant/child 1 breath every 2–3 seconds (~20–30/min).
Give each breath over about 1 second, to visible chest rise, avoiding over-ventilation.
Reassess the pulse about every 2 minutes (≤10 seconds).
If the pulse is lost, begin CPR immediately, starting with compressions, and apply an AED.
If breathing recovers adequately, monitor and place in the recovery position as appropriate.

algorithms.title: Ventilation With an Advanced Airway
algorithms.description: How ventilation changes once a supraglottic device or endotracheal tube is placed during cardiac arrest.
steps: Continue chest compressions continuously at 100–120/min — do not pause for breaths.
Give each ventilation gently to visible chest rise; avoid over-ventilation.
Confirm and monitor placement with clinical signs and waveform capnography where available.
Reassess the rhythm at the scheduled AED/monitor intervals and continue until ROSC or handoff.
